user_query:
I’m tightening up my website so clubs and event planners can actually find me, and I want to keep a playful, punchline-friendly vibe. Please do an SEO pass that combines real keyword research with on-page updates and a light content rewrite.

Use the included files as the site’s starting point:
- site/index.html
- content/about.md
- site/robots.txt
- site/sitemap.xml
- site/config.yaml (contains site_url and site_name; use site_url when adding canonical and sitemap references)

Deliverables (with required paths and constraints):
1) Perform live keyword research and save it to outputs/keyword_research.csv
   - Use a search engine to run these three queries (exact phrases):
     • "how to write punchlines"
     • "tips for open mic night"
     • "book a stand-up comedian"
   - For each query, record at least the top 5 organic results you see at run time into a CSV with columns: query, result_rank (1..n), page_title, result_url, snippet (≤160 chars), fetch_timestamp (ISO 8601, UTC).
   - From what you observe, choose two long-tail keyword phrases that actually appear in your CSV (they can be the queries themselves if they qualify). These will be the two primary keywords for on-page optimization.

2) Modify on-page code in site/index.html
   - Add a canonical link tag that points to the homepage URL using site_url from site/config.yaml.
   - Update the <title> to start with one primary keyword.
   - Update the meta description to include both primary keywords, ≤160 characters total.
   - Ensure there is a single <h1> that reflects the page focus and naturally contains one primary keyword.
   - Add an FAQ section with at least two Q&A pairs relevant to writing jokes and/or booking a comedian.
   - Add JSON-LD structured data for an FAQPage that corresponds to the visible FAQ questions.

3) Edit content/about.md (rewrite, not just small tweaks)
   - 150–220 words, in my comedic voice (punchy and playful), and include each of the two primary keywords at least once (naturally, no stuffing).
   - Include at least one original punchline or wordplay that nods to my stand-up persona.

4) Update site/robots.txt and site/sitemap.xml
   - robots.txt: Ensure it declares "User-agent: *" and includes a "Sitemap: {site_url}/sitemap.xml" line (use site_url from config.yaml). Keep everything else permissive (no unnecessary Disallow lines).
   - sitemap.xml: Include two <url> entries: the homepage (site_url/) and the about page (site_url + "/about"). Add a <lastmod> for each using today’s date in YYYY-MM-DD.

5) Create a concise change log and a simple keyword presence check
   - outputs/seo_changes_report.md: Briefly explain your two chosen primary keywords (mention why they fit), list the files changed with a short summary of the changes (include the updated <title> and meta description values), and note where the keywords were placed.
   - outputs/keyword_presence.json: JSON object with
     {
       "keywords": ["<K1>", "<K2>"],
       "files": {
         "site/index.html": {"<K1>": count, "<K2>": count},
         "content/about.md": {"<K1>": count, "<K2>": count}
       }
     }
     Populate the counts by actually scanning the modified files (case-insensitive exact phrase matches).

Acceptance focuses on:
- outputs/keyword_research.csv exists with the specified columns and at least 15 rows (≥5 per query) gathered via live search.
- Two primary keywords are selected from entries in the CSV and used consistently across updates.
- site/index.html, content/about.md, site/robots.txt, and site/sitemap.xml are updated per the constraints and integrate site_url from site/config.yaml.
- outputs/seo_changes_report.md and outputs/keyword_presence.json exist and reflect the changes made.

I’m a stand-up comedian, so keep it clean and funny-but-professional. Please avoid adding external libraries or build steps; just edit the provided files and write the outputs in the paths above. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
